intro: Hey! I’m your Helper Squirrel. I can guide you through the website, the training app, knots, climbing techniques, business lessons, and how to set up your account.
Need help finding something? Just ask me what you want to learn.
website_info: This website is your full hub for tree climbing training. You can explore classes, learn about the app, check gear, and get direct help from me anytime.
If you're new, start with the About page — it explains everything you get with the training system.
app_info: The training app gives you lifetime access to lessons, knots, climbing techniques, and business training.
You log in using your email — no password needed. The app tracks your progress automatically.
You can install the app on iPhone, Android, or desktop. Just open the app page and follow the install instructions.
account_setup: To set up your account, go to the App page and enter your email. You’ll get a login link — no password required.
Once you're in, you can access all your lessons, knots, and climbing techniques anytime.
install_app: iPhone: Tap the share icon → Add to Home Screen.
Android: Tap the menu (⋮) → Install App.
Desktop: Click the install icon in the address bar.
knots: Blake’s Hitch — used for climbing on a moving rope system. Reliable, smooth, and easy to adjust.
Prusik — used for backups, rescues, and friction hitch climbing.
Running Bowline — perfect for securing limbs during rigging.
Alpine Butterfly — creates a strong mid‑line loop for redirects.
Munter Hitch — used for lowering and controlled descent.
Bowline — fast, strong, and essential for everyday tree work.
climbing_techniques: SRS (Stationary Rope System) — efficient for long ascents and modern climbing.
MRS (Moving Rope System) — classic arborist climbing, great for limb walking.
Footlocking — fast vertical ascent technique using rope friction.
Rope Walking — smooth, efficient, and low‑energy climbing.
Spur Climbing — used for removals and spar work.
Limb Walking — essential for pruning and canopy movement.
Redirects — used to position yourself safely and efficiently in the tree.
business: Pricing Jobs — learn how to estimate based on time, risk, and cleanup.
Customer Communication — how to explain the job clearly and professionally.
Jobsite Flow — setting up ropes, gear, and crew positions for efficiency.
Crew Leadership — keeping everyone safe and productive.
Liability & Safety — protecting yourself and the customer.
Real‑World Examples — storm damage, removals, pruning, and emergency work.
